system: you are an expert programmer bioinformatician with expertise in nextflow pipelines
context: the current project is a nextflow pipeline that allows the user to select one of 3 tools for barcode calling
the pipeline was developed to run a benchmarking of the 3 tools on simulated and real data
The current version work well with the profile slurm for HPC systems and with the combined profiles local,singularity
the project include the job submission files with corresponding paramter json files needed to reproduce the benchmarking results
the benchmark was done using barcodes and simulated reads with different error rates and barcode lengths as well as with real barcode sequencing data
the datasets, simulated and real, have been deposited in zenodo
tasks: we need to clean the repository, remove all files that are not needed to reproduce the benchmarking results
add intructions  to reproduce/run the benchmarking analyses
the instuction should include the software requirements (i.e nextflow, singularity, conda, etc.) and insturction to downaload the data from zenod and place it in a specific directory

create scripts to generate all jobs and params files for the benchamariking analysis, be sure to understand how the jobs are run, each nextflow job should work in a separted directory to avoid lock conflicts
As each benchmark has different parametesr we need to create a script that will generate the jobs and params files for each benchmark
ideally the script should take the paramters including paths of the input files and output .sh job submission scripts together with the json paramater files
the datasets used for the benchmark should be downloaded from zenodo and placed in a specific directory
the scripts should take the inputs from the downloaded datasets and use them to run the benchmarking analysis
the scripts should be named like generate_jobs_and_params_<benchmark_name>.py
the scripts should be executable and should be placed in the bin directory
the notebooks to analyze the benchmarking results should be placed in a notebooks directory inside the project directory

parameter_sweep.description: we evaluate the performance of the 3 tools under different parameters to identify the optimal parameters for each tool

randomBarcodes.description: for 30, 32 and 34 only nthesh was swept, for 28 and 36 nt barcodes ntriage and nthresh were swept

quik.description: for 30, 32 and 34 only rejection_threshold r was swept, for 28 and 36 nt barcodes strategy and rejection_threshold r were swept

columba.description: Only identity threshold I was swept for all barcodes from 28 to 36 nt

runtime_benchmarks.description: we evaluate the runtime performance of the 3 tools under different resource configurations

quik.description: for 36 nt barcodes strategy and rejection_threshold were swept

error_rate_benchmarks.description: we evaluate the performance of the 3 tools under different error rates

Scaling_benchmarks.description: we evaluate the performance of the 3 tools on 1 million reads, all previous benchmarks were done on 200k reads

Real_data_benchmarks.description: we evaluate the performance of the 3 tools on real data from 3 different samples (arrays) with expected different error rates. To calculate the precision a decoy set of barcodes was used for each sample. The assignment of the decoy barcodes was considered false positives and used to estimate the precision

200k_set.description: it includes the barcodes, answers (ground truth), decoy barcodes (set2 barcodes) and simulated reads (low, medium, high error rates) for the 200k benchmark

1M_set.description: it includes the barcodes, answers (ground truth), decoy barcodes (set2 barcodes) and simulated reads (low, medium, high error rates) for the 1 million reads benchmark

reald_data_set.description: it includes the barcodes, answers (ground truth), decoy barcodes (set2 barcodes) and simulated reads (low, medium, high error rates) for the real data benchmark
